---
title: "Local Sensitivity Analysis"

date: last-modified

  ![](../resources/esqlabs.svg){height=100}

---

::: {.r-fit-text}
## **🎯 Goal: Learn how to identify the most influential parameters on the model output.**
:::



::: {.cell}

:::


## Sensitivity Analysis in PK-Sim

![](https://881660647-files.gitbook.io/~/files/v0/b/gitbook-legacy-files/o/assets%2F-LNl6UIiFP7k0sNQthlR%2F-LOWPv3bS-p6gLjXajFs%2F-LOWQ5HAkIoayhFuXldn%2FSA_Parameters1.png?generation=1539232796810927&alt=media)

. . .

:::center-x
Also available using `{ospsuite}` !
:::


## Overview

1. Setup sensitivity analysis
2. Run sensitivity analysis
3. Explore results
4. Import & Export

# Preparation

## Load `{ospsuite}`


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ospsuite)
```
:::


## Load Sample Simulation


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
sim <- loadSimulation(system.file(
  "extdata",
  "Aciclovir.pkml",
  package = "ospsuite"
))
clearOutputs(sim)
addOutputs(
  "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)",
  sim
)
```
:::


# Setup sensitivity analysis

## Workflow

Steps:

  1. Define parameters to be varied
  2. Create `SensitivityAnalysis` object
  3. Setup parameter variations
  
:::notes

- The parameters to vary, that we selected (default: all suitable parameters, here 228)
- The range of variation, that we defined (default: 10%)
- The number of steps, that we defined (default: 2)

Then, the sensitivity analysis will be performed, and it will give us an idea on how much different PK parameters, such as Cmax or AUC, will vary along with the parameters we selected.

Previously, we defined a variation of 20%, once, for the fraction unbound, lipophilicity and liver volume. We will see how much these variations impact the PK parameters.
:::

## Define parameters to be varied

Specific parameters to vary can be defined selectively.  

If no parameters are specified, all constant and suitable parameters are automatically varied.

Use `potentialVariableParametersFor()` to get a list of all suitable parameters.



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
params <- potentialVariableParameterPathsFor(sim)
params
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}



:::
:::


## Create `SensitivityAnalysis` object

With all potential parameters:


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
sa <- SensitivityAnalysis$new(simulation = sim, parameterPaths = params)
sa
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<SensitivityAnalysis>
  • Number of steps: 2
  • Variation range: 0.1
  • Number of parameters to vary: 323
```


:::
:::


## Select Parameters

With only some parameters:


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
sa <- SensitivityAnalysis$new(
  simulation = sim,
  parameterPaths = c(
    "Aciclovir|Lipophilicity",
    "Aciclovir|Fraction unbound (plasma, reference value)"
  )
)
sa
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<SensitivityAnalysis>
  • Number of steps: 2
  • Variation range: 0.1
  • Number of parameters to vary: 2
```


:::
:::



## Add Parameters Afterwards

:::{.callout-important}
Supplementary parameters can be added to an existing `SensitivityAnalysis`.
:::


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
sa$addParameterPaths("Organism|Liver|Volume")

sa
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<SensitivityAnalysis>
  • Number of steps: 2
  • Variation range: 0.1
  • Number of parameters to vary: 3
```


:::
:::


:::{.callout-warning}
On an analysis created **without** `parameterPaths` (all-params default), `addParameterPaths()` switches the SA to vary **only** the newly added parameters.
:::


## Setup parameter variations

By default, parameters are incremented/decremented by 10%, twice.

But this can be customized: Here, we will vary the parameters by 20% once.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
# One variation per direction (default: 2)
sa$numberOfSteps <- 1

# Variation range (default: 0.1 = 10%)
sa$variationRange <- 0.2

sa
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<SensitivityAnalysis>
  • Number of steps: 1
  • Variation range: 0.2
  • Number of parameters to vary: 3
```


:::
:::



. . .

::: {.callout-important}
Settings are applied to all parameters.
:::

. . .

::: {.callout-note}
`variationRange` is **symmetric**: `0.2` varies each parameter from `-20%` to `+20%` around its baseline.

Total simulations per run = `2 * numberOfSteps * length(parameterPaths)`.
:::


# Run Sensitivity Analysis

## Execute

Simply execute:


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
saResult <- runSensitivityAnalysis(sa)

saResult
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<SensitivityAnalysisResults>
  • Number of calculated sensitivities: 33
Available PK Parameters:
  • C_max
  • t_max
  • C_tEnd
  • AUC_tEnd
  • AUC_inf
  • MRT
  • Thalf
  • FractionAucLastToInf
  • CL
  • Vss
  • Vd
For the following outputs:
  • Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)
```


:::
:::


. . .

:::{.callout-tip}
## Add Run options with `SensitivityAnalysisRunOptions`

- `numberOfCores`
- `showProgress`


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
runSensitivityAnalysis(
  sa,
  sensitivityAnalysisRunOptions = SensitivityAnalysisRunOptions$new(
    numberOfCores = 2,
    showProgress = TRUE
  )
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<SensitivityAnalysisResults>
  • Number of calculated sensitivities: 33
Available PK Parameters:
  • C_max
  • t_max
  • C_tEnd
  • AUC_tEnd
  • AUC_inf
  • MRT
  • Thalf
  • FractionAucLastToInf
  • CL
  • Vss
  • Vd
For the following outputs:
  • Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)
```


:::
:::

:::


# Explore Sensitivity Analysis Results

## List Available Results


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
saResult$allQuantityPaths
```

```
[1] "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)"
```

. . .


```
 [1] "C_max"                "t_max"                "C_tEnd"              
 [4] "AUC_tEnd"             "AUC_inf"              "MRT"
```

:::
:::




## Retrieve results

```{.r .cell-code}
outputPath <- saResult$allQuantityPaths[[1]]
saResult$allPKParameterSensitivitiesFor(
  pkParameterName = "C_max",
  outputPath = outputPath
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[[1]]

  Venous Blood)

[[2]]

```

```{.r .cell-code}
  pkParameterName = "AUC_inf",
  outputPath = outputPath
)
```

```
[[1]]
<PKParameterSensitivity>
  • Parameter name: Aciclovir-Fraction unbound (plasma, reference value)
  • PK-Parameter: AUC_inf
  • Output path: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral
  Venous Blood)
```

:::
:::



## Sensitivity Threshold

By default, only the most important parameters are returned: those contributing 90% of the total sensitivity.

Adjust via the `totalSensitivityThreshold` argument of `allPKParameterSensitivitiesFor()` (default `0.9`).

Set `totalSensitivityThreshold = 1` to return all parameters.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
saResult$allPKParameterSensitivitiesFor(
  pkParameterName = "AUC_inf",
  outputPath = outputPath,
  totalSensitivityThreshold = 1
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[[1]]
<PKParameterSensitivity>
  • Parameter name: Aciclovir-Fraction unbound (plasma, reference value)
  • PK-Parameter: AUC_inf
  • Output path: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral
  Venous Blood)
  • Value: -0.805464213439978

[[2]]
<PKParameterSensitivity>
  • Parameter name: Aciclovir-Lipophilicity
  • PK-Parameter: AUC_inf
  • Output path: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral
  Venous Blood)
  • Value: 0.00461781402448199

[[3]]
<PKParameterSensitivity>
  • Parameter name: Liver-Volume
  • PK-Parameter: AUC_inf
  • Output path: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral
  Venous Blood)
  • Value: 0
```


:::
:::


## Interpret Results

Sensitivity definition:

$$S_{Y,X} = \frac{\Delta Y / Y}{\Delta X / X}$$

Relative change in PK parameter `Y` per relative change in model parameter `X`.

Examples:

- Variation range set to 0.1 (10%) for parameter X
- Returned value of `-1` as sensitivity for parameter Y

A variation of 10 % of parameter X leads to a decrease (minus sign) of Y by 10 % (-1 * 10%)

- Variation range set to 0.2 (20%) for parameter X
- Returned value of `0.25` as sensitivity for parameter Y

A variation of 20 % of parameter X leads to an increase (positive) of Y by 5 % (0.25 * 20%)


## Single Sensitivity Value


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
saResult$pkParameterSensitivityValueFor(
  pkParameterName = "C_max",
  parameterName = "Aciclovir-Fraction unbound (plasma, reference value)",
  outputPath = outputPath
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] -0.7415616
```


:::
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

:::


. . . 

-0.7415616 means that a 20% increase (defined variation range of 0.2) of the fraction unbound in plasma leads to a -15% (-74% * 20%) decrease of $C_{max}$ at Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood).


# Import & Export

## CSV Round-Trip


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Export
exportSensitivityAnalysisResultsToCSV(
  results = saResult,
  filePath = "mySAResults.csv"
)
```
:::


. . . 


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Import
importedSAResult <- importSensitivityAnalysisResultsFromCSV(
  filePaths = "mySAResults.csv",
  simulation = sim
)
```
:::


. . . 

:::{.callout-important}
Original Simulation is required to import sensitivity analysis results.
:::


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

:::


# Exercise

## Exercise Overview

- Create a new sensitivity analysis from scratch
- Select 5 parameters (include Dose and Fraction unbound)
- Run with defaults, then with `variationRange = 0.2`
- Compare results for `C_max` and `CL`

## Create the Analysis

Create a new sensitivity analysis from scratch. Select 5 parameters of your choice. Include the Dose and the fraction unbound.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
sim <- loadSimulation(system.file(
  "extdata",
  "Aciclovir.pkml",
  package = "ospsuite"
))

sa <- SensitivityAnalysis$new(
  simulation = sim,
  parameterPaths = c(
    "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose",
    "Aciclovir|Fraction unbound (plasma, reference value)",
    "Organism|Spleen|Volume",
    "Organism|Protein ratio (interstitial/plasma)",
    "Organism|VenousBlood|Volume"
  )
)
```
:::



## Run Two Analyses

Compare two sensitivity analyses. Keep steps and variation range as default for the first, set `variationRange` to 0.2 for the second, same parameters. Run both.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
sa20 <- SensitivityAnalysis$new(
  simulation = sim,
  parameterPaths = c(
    "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose",
    "Aciclovir|Fraction unbound (plasma, reference value)",
    "Organism|Spleen|Volume",
    "Organism|Protein ratio (interstitial/plasma)",
    "Organism|VenousBlood|Volume"
  )
)

sa20$variationRange <- 0.2

saResult <- runSensitivityAnalysis(sa)
saResult20 <- runSensitivityAnalysis(sa20)
```
:::


## Compare `C_max`

Compare both results for `C_max`. Display all parameters (`totalSensitivityThreshold = 1`).


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
outputPath <- saResult$allQuantityPaths[[1]]
outputPath20 <- saResult20$allQuantityPaths[[1]]

# Let's look at the results for Cmax
saResult$allPKParameterSensitivitiesFor(
  pkParameterName = "C_max",
  outputPath = outputPath,
  totalSensitivityThreshold = 1
)
saResult20$allPKParameterSensitivitiesFor(
  pkParameterName = "C_max",
  outputPath = outputPath20,
  totalSensitivityThreshold = 1
)
```
:::


## Compare `CL`


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
# Clearance results
saResult$allPKParameterSensitivitiesFor(
  pkParameterName = "CL",
  outputPath = outputPath,
  totalSensitivityThreshold = 1
)
saResult20$allPKParameterSensitivitiesFor(
  pkParameterName = "CL",
  outputPath = outputPath20,
  totalSensitivityThreshold = 1
)
```
:::


## Interpretation

How do you interpret the results?

::: {.callout-tip collapse="true"}
## Solution

- `C_max` vs `Dose` is (almost) 1 in both cases: a 10% increase in `Dose` leads to a 10% increase in `C_max`, same for 20%
- `CL` vs `Fraction unbound` is around 0.68 in both cases: 10% increase of `Fraction unbound` → 10% * 68% = 6.8% increase in `CL`; 20% → 20% * 68% = 13.6% increase

Sensitivity analysis is "local" and does not consider non-linearities — expect slightly different results for different variation ranges.
:::

## Resources

- [PK Sim section about Sensitivity Analysis](https://docs.open-systems-pharmacology.org/shared-tools-and-example-workflows/sensitivity-analysis)
- [`{ospsuite}` vignette](https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/articles/sensitivity-analysis.html)

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
